description: Canonical terminology registry for Hollow House Institute governance standards.

terms.definition: The principle that humans retain final decision authority and ethical accountability in AI-mediated systems.
A non-bypassable mechanism allowing a human operator to halt system execution.
A defined threshold where automated system output requires human review or intervention.
A governance architecture where human oversight is integrated into system decision processes.
The set of controls governing how AI systems operate during real-time execution.
The structural control layer responsible for enforcing system policies and operational constraints.
The hierarchical architecture of governance standards, policies, and enforcement mechanisms.
The structured rule system limiting permissible system behaviors.
Runtime restrictions applied to prevent unsafe or unauthorized system actions.
Controlled data pathways governing how information moves through systems.
The canonical source defining the meaning of governance terminology.
The requirement that systems reference canonical definitions rather than redefining terms locally.
The preservation of stable meaning across governance documentation and systems.
Gradual alteration of term meaning across documentation or system implementations.
Ongoing verification that systems remain compliant with governance rules.
A documented piece of evidence supporting governance compliance verification.
A traceable record of system decisions and governance interventions.
A compiled collection of artifacts demonstrating system compliance.
Independent confirmation that governance mechanisms operate correctly.
A system structured to allow immediate compliance evaluation.
The operational limits within which a system remains compliant with governance rules.
A governance condition where monitoring or intervention mechanisms fail.
The condition in which system operations remain unaltered and trustworthy.
The ability of governance mechanisms to remain effective during system stress or change.
The ability of a system to maintain functional operation during disruptions.
